title: Tarte Normande
subtitle: Norman apple tart with almond cream and sweet pastry
excerpt: Tarte Normande is an apple tart from Normandy filled with a rich almond cream poured over sliced apples in a blind-baked sweet pastry case. The almond cream sets around the apples during baking. A small amount of calvados or brandy in the filling is traditional. It is served at room temperature and keeps well for two days.
sourceNotes: Tarte Normande is a classic of Norman patisserie, documented in French culinary sources from the nineteenth century and associated with the apple orchards of Normandy and the regional apple brandy calvados. The almond cream filling sets it apart from a plain apple tart. This recipe uses brandy as a widely available substitute for calvados, which may not be readily available outside France. This recipe is synthesised from the established Norman tart tradition.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry case can be blind-baked a day ahead. Assemble and bake on the day for best results.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan or conventional; the almond cream should be set and golden across the surface

glossaryTerms.definition: The almond cream for tarte Normande (creme normande) is a loose mixture of eggs, sugar, double cream, and ground almonds. It is not the same as frangipane, which is richer and more like a cake batter. The normande cream is poured over the apple slices and should just cover them. It sets to a soft, slightly chewy layer during baking. If it looks liquid when you take the tart from the oven, give it 5 more minutes. Once cooled it will firm up further.

content.text: The tart has three stages: make and chill the pastry, blind bake the case, then fill and bake. The

is poured over the raw apple slices in the baked case. The apples soften in the oven while the cream sets around them. Slice the apples thinly so they cook through; thick wedges will still be firm when the cream is done.

items.prepNote: juice, to coat the apple slices

content.text: Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub the 100 g cold butter into the 200 g flour and 60 g icing sugar until it resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolk, salt, and 1 to 2 tablespoons cold water and mix until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat the oven to 190 degrees C. Roll the pastry to 3 mm and line a 23 cm tart tin. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Line with paper and baking beans and blind bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and bake for 5 more minutes until the base is dry and very pale gold.

Toss the apple slices in the lemon juice. Arrange them in overlapping concentric circles in the baked case. Whisk together the 2 eggs, 60 g caster sugar, 150 ml double cream, 60 g ground almonds and brandy if using until smooth. Pour the almond cream over the apples; it should just cover them. Bake at 190 degrees C for 28 to 32 minutes until the cream is set and golden across the surface, and the apples are tender when tested with a knife tip. Cool for at least 30 minutes. Dust with icing sugar before serving.

items.problem: Almond cream is still liquid in the centre when the edges look set
items.solution: Cover the edges loosely with foil to prevent overbrowning and return to the oven for 5 to 8 minutes. The centre will always set slightly later than the edges.

items.problem: Apple slices are still firm when the filling is done
items.solution: The apples were cut too thick. Slice them no more than 4 mm thick and fan them out so they form a single overlapping layer rather than stacking.
